idea-plugin.name: Whisper
idea-plugin.description:
Voice-to-text dictation for JetBrains IDEs — powered by OpenAI Whisper.

Stop typing. Start talking. Whisper turns your voice into text directly inside the IDE, in any focused field — editor, terminal, search, commit message, AI chat panels, anywhere.

Highlights

  • Three backends — Local (free, fully offline via whisper.cpp), OpenAI Whisper API, or Groq (Whisper Large V3, fastest).
  • Three dictation modes — Dictate (raw transcription), Code (spoken punctuation → symbols, filler words stripped, casing fixed), Command (formatted as a clean coding instruction for AI chat).
  • Inserts anywhere — direct caret insertion in the editor, clipboard-paste fallback for tool windows and external apps.
  • Multilingual — supports every language Whisper does (en, fr, de, es, it, pt, ja, zh, …).
  • Zero cloud by default — Local backend runs entirely on your machine. Audio never leaves your computer unless you opt in to a cloud backend.
  • One-shortcut workflow — press, speak, press again. Status bar shows recording state.

Quick start

  1. Install sox (brew install sox) or ffmpeg for audio capture.
  2. Run Whisper: Setup Local Transcription to install whisper.cpp + a model (~150 MB).
  3. Press ⌘M (macOS) / Ctrl+M (Windows/Linux) to start. Press again to stop and insert.

Configuration

Settings → Tools → Whisper. Configure backend, dictation mode, language, API keys, and recording tool.

Inspired by superwhisper.com — built into your IDE, free, and open.
